title: Prejob Guidance * Women - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Prejob Guidance * Women 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Native American Community Women No Filter African American Community Native American Community Women Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides work clothing and employment support services. Work-Ready Clothing: Interview and work-ready clothing to help individuals transition into the workforce. Clients receive five pieces of work-ready clothing plus shoes, a coat, and accessories when they have a job interview, and five additional pieces of clothing when they get a job. Job Preparation: Provides job preparation classes for job seekers called Fast Track. Classes are currently online only. The regular cycle of four classes repeats monthly January-November. Additional services are available once Fast Track is completed. Employment Retention: The Professional Women's Group (PWG) is an ongoing group providing the opportunity for individuals to virtually meet with working women. Includes presentations and workshops to support life and career. The PWG meets regularly for programming with speakers from the community. Examples of program themes include financial education and planning, career development, the written and unwritten rules of the workplace, health and wellness, communication, and leadership skills. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Women and gender expansive individuals referred by a member agency Hours: Services: By appointment Job preparation: Thursday 10am-11:30am Email (503) 249-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON TRADESWOMEN TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 7-week, state-certified, pre-apprenticeship training class in the skilled construction trades that provides students with the following: -Learn basic trades, math, and measurement -Learn to use hand and power tools -Receive an introduction to green building -Explore topics such as job site safety and construction culture -Gain 30 hours of hands-on experience working alongside skilled female instructors on actual job sites -Field trips to apprenticeship training centers and active construction sites -Improve physical fitness with strength training taught by a certified fitness trainer Career counselors assist program graduates with job searches and application processes to apprenticeship training programs upon graduation. Career counselors also provide individualized employment counseling throughout the entire course of the graduate's trades career. 16790 NE Mason Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: -Women and other gender minorities -Age 18 years or older -GED or high school diploma -Ability to pass a drug test -Attend an information session -Take the National Career Readiness Certificate (NCRC) test Additional enrollment requirements may be required and are available during the initial informational session Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (503) 335-8200 121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON OREGON TRADESWOMEN TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS
